# coreweave_sandbox_managed_runner (Resource)

Manage a CoreWeave Sandbox platform-managed runner. A managed runner installs the sandbox runtime onto a CKS cluster and binds one or more profile templates that sandboxes can launch against.

Concurrent updates to the same runner are not safe â€” serialize mutations client-side if multiple operators may apply Terraform at the same time.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "coreweave_sandbox_profile_template" "default" {
  display_name = "default-cpu"
  description  = "Default CPU profile for sandboxes on this runner."

  spec = {
    runtime_class = "kata-qemu"
    resource_defaults = {
      cpu_request    = "500m"
      memory_request = "1Gi"
      cpu_limit      = "2"
      memory_limit   = "4Gi"
    }
  }
}

resource "coreweave_sandbox_managed_runner" "prod_east" {
  runner_id    = "prod-east-managed"
  display_name = "Prod East (managed)"
  zone         = "US-EAST-04A"
  cluster_name = "prod-east"

  release_channel         = "RELEASE_CHANNEL_STABLE"
  enforce_resource_limits = true

  maintenance_policy = {
    windows = [
      {
        cron             = "0 2 * * SAT"
        duration_seconds = 7200
      }
    ]
  }

  overrides = {
    node_selector = {
      "workload-class" = "general"
    }
    resources = {
      cpu_request    = "2"
      memory_request = "4Gi"
      cpu_limit      = "4"
      memory_limit   = "8Gi"
    }
    scaling = {
      autoscaling_enabled = true
      min_replicas        = 1
      max_replicas        = 5
    }
  }

  profile_bindings = [
    {
      profile_template_id = coreweave_sandbox_profile_template.default.id
      profile_name        = "default"
      is_default          = true
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `profile_bindings` (Attributes Set) Profile templates attached to this runner. Must contain at least one entry, and exactly one entry must have `is_default = true`. Bindings are matched by `profile_template_id` on update; the desired list fully replaces the current list within one transaction. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--profile_bindings))
- `runner_id` (String) Operator-assigned runner identifier (unique within the organization). Immutable after create.
- `zone` (String) Geographic zone (e.g., `US-EAST-04A`).

### Optional

- `allow_privileged_profile_annotations` (Boolean) When true, profile templates bound to this runner may carry pod annotations from prefix categories the platform otherwise restricts. Contact CoreWeave support before enabling. Defaults to `false`.
- `cluster_id` (String) CKS cluster UUID the runner installs into. Exactly one of `cluster_id` or `cluster_name` must be set; the other is resolved on read. Immutable after create.
- `cluster_name` (String) CKS cluster display name the runner installs into. Exactly one of `cluster_id` or `cluster_name` must be set; the other is resolved on read. Immutable after create.
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable name for the runner.
- `enforce_resource_limits` (Boolean) When true, sandboxes and bound profile templates must declare both memory and CPU limits. Defaults to `false`.
- `maintenance_policy` (Attributes) Maintenance windows and exclusion periods controlling when updates may be applied. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_policy))
- `overrides` (Attributes) Customer-settable overrides for the runner deployment. All fields are optional; unset fields fall back to platform defaults. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides))
- `release_channel` (String) Release channel for automatic updates. One of `RELEASE_CHANNEL_STABLE`, `RELEASE_CHANNEL_RAPID`. Defaults to `RELEASE_CHANNEL_STABLE`.
- `runner_group_id` (String) Runner group ID for scheduling affinity.

### Read-Only

- `active_config_version` (String) Active configuration version running on the runner.
- `active_profile_spec_version` (String) Active profile spec version.
- `active_revision` (Number) Active revision number currently running.
- `connection_status` (String) Live connection status to Gateway.
- `created_at` (String)
- `deployment_spec` (Attributes) Resolved server-side deployment spec for the runner, combining version defaults, org-level policies, and customer overrides. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deployment_spec))
- `id` (String) Server-assigned UUID for the runner.
- `install_error` (Attributes) Populated when `install_status == RUNNER_INSTALL_STATUS_FAILED`. Cleared when status transitions back to provisioning or ready. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_error))
- `install_status` (String) Current installation lifecycle status.
- `last_heartbeat_at` (String)
- `management_mode` (String) Always `MANAGEMENT_MODE_MANAGED` for this resource.
- `rollout_in_progress` (Boolean) True when the runner is mid-rollout (`target_revision > active_revision`).
- `target_revision` (Number) Target revision number that should be rolled out.
- `update_available` (Boolean) True when a newer version is available on the runner's release channel.
- `updated_at` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--profile_bindings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `profile_bindings`

Required:

- `profile_template_id` (String) ID of the profile template to attach.

Optional:

- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this is the default profile for the runner. Exactly one binding must set this to `true`.
- `overrides_json` (String) Per-binding overrides as a JSON-encoded `ProfileSpec` fragment. Must be canonical JSON; use `jsonencode({...})` to construct.
- `profile_name` (String) Profile name on this runner. Overrides the template's name when set.


<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_policy`

Optional:

- `exclusions` (Attributes List) One-time exclusion periods during which no updates may be applied. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_policy--exclusions))
- `windows` (Attributes List) Recurring time windows during which updates are allowed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--maintenance_policy--windows))

<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_policy--exclusions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_policy.exclusions`

Required:

- `end_time` (String) RFC3339 end of the exclusion.
- `start_time` (String) RFC3339 start of the exclusion.

Optional:

- `reason` (String) Human-readable reason.


<a id="nestedatt--maintenance_policy--windows"></a>
### Nested Schema for `maintenance_policy.windows`

Required:

- `cron` (String) Cron expression for the window start (e.g., `0 2 * * SAT`).
- `duration_seconds` (Number) Duration of the window in seconds.



<a id="nestedatt--overrides"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Additional annotations on runner pods.
- `args` (List of String) Additional command-line arguments.
- `cpu_runtime_class` (String) Runtime class for CPU-only sandbox pods.
- `env` (Map of String) Additional environment variables.
- `gpu_runtime_class` (String) Runtime class for GPU sandbox pods.
- `labels` (Map of String) Additional labels on runner pods.
- `node_selector` (Map of String) Node selector labels for the runner pods.
- `resources` (Attributes) Resource requirements override for the runner pod. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides--resources))
- `scaling` (Attributes) Scaling configuration override. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides--scaling))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations applied to the runner pods. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--overrides--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--overrides--resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides.resources`

Optional:

- `cpu_limit` (String) CPU limit (e.g., `2`).
- `cpu_request` (String) CPU request (e.g., `500m`).
- `memory_limit` (String) Memory limit (e.g., `4Gi`).
- `memory_request` (String) Memory request (e.g., `1Gi`).


<a id="nestedatt--overrides--scaling"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides.scaling`

Optional:

- `autoscaling_enabled` (Boolean) Whether autoscaling is enabled.
- `max_replicas` (Number) Maximum replicas when autoscaling.
- `min_replicas` (Number) Minimum replicas when autoscaling.
- `replicas` (Number) Number of replicas (when autoscaling is disabled).


<a id="nestedatt--overrides--tolerations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `overrides.tolerations`

Optional:

- `effect` (String)
- `key` (String)
- `operator` (String)
- `value` (String)

Read-Only:

- `gateway_server` (String)
- `gpu_runtime_class` (String)
- `image` (String)
- `image_pull_policy` (String)

- `labels` (Map of String)
- `node_selector` (Map of String)
- `resources` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deployment_spec--resources))
- `scaling` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deployment_spec--scaling))
- `service_account` (String)
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deployment_spec--tolerations))

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = coreweave_sandbox_managed_runner.prod_east
  id = "{{id}}"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import coreweave_sandbox_managed_runner.prod_east {{id}}
```
